result:
I'd delete the neural pattern that lets humans dehumanize each other.

Not empathy itself, but that specific switch—quiet, almost invisible—that says “those people don’t count.” Once that pattern is gone, every invention, border, or belief would still be ours to argue over, but it would become literally impossible to build systems that shrug at another’s pain. War wouldn’t vanish overnight, no fairytale peace, yet every new cruelty would now register inside the enactor’s skull like a hammer on bone. Genocides, sweatshops, pogroms, casual indifference to strangers on the street—they’d all short-circuit at the source.

The rest of the world could stay flawed, chaotic, beautifully human. We’d still disagree, still compete, still screw up. But zeroing out the ability to erase one another from moral accounting means whatever comes next would have to pass through the undiluted recognition that the other is also me.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
